dc_description: Color landscape photograph taken from a cyclist’s perspective on a paved roadside. The lower foreground is occupied by an out-of-focus bicycle handlebar, brake hood, and small digital device. At right, a blue road sign on two metal posts indicates directions to Bracciano via SS493 Braccianese Claudia, and to Cassia/SS2 and Viterbo. A roadway with several cars extends into the background, bordered by trees, dry grass, sidewalk paving, and a clear blue sky.
novel_visual_observations: The sign places the cyclist at a northern route decision point outside Rome, with Bracciano to the left and Cassia/Viterbo to the right.
The bicycle cockpit and absence of a visible bike lane emphasize self-navigation beside ordinary motor traffic rather than protected cycling infrastructure.
storytelling_narrative: With my hands on the taped bars, I watched the blue sign divide the road: Bracciano bending left, Cassia and Viterbo slipping right. The sun hardened every edge, even my rented confidence. Cars passed in a language I still translated with my shoulders. I was leaving Rome, yet already learning the social weight of where a bicycle may lean, how foreignness can begin in the body, slowly under my skin.
